key_insight: Increasing the barrel filling ratio from 43% to 53% produced a denser detonation-sprayed Cr3C2–NiCr coating with better wear resistance, especially under higher load.
engineering_relevance: Optimizing spray parameters can improve the durability of carbide-based protective coatings for components exposed to severe friction, wear, and high-temperature service.
method: Detonation-sprayed Cr3C2–NiCr coatings were deposited at two barrel filling ratios: 43% and 53%.
Microstructure and phase composition were analyzed to assess porosity, carbide distribution, and carbide decarburization.
Dry sliding tribology tests were run in a ball-on-disc setup at 10 and 15 N and 5 and 10 cm/s.
evidence_signal: The 53% filling-ratio coating achieved the lowest wear rate, 1.23 × 10^-4 mm3/(m·N), and showed improved performance under elevated load despite a higher coefficient of friction.
watch_item: Higher barrel filling ratio improved density, but the friction increase suggests a tradeoff that should be validated for specific operating loads and counterface conditions.
paper_title: Effect of Barrel Filling Ratio on the Microstructure, Phase Composition and Tribological Performance of Detonation-Sprayed Cr3C2–NiCr Coatings
